title: "__last_updated__" is not working

posts.author: madhusudhana.kolli (Madhu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi ,
May I know what is the wrong with below query.
It was working couple of days back and it’s started throwing an issue.

Also, this is an example query provided on monday.com documentation.

GraphQL API

Last updated

The last updated column represents when an item was last updated. You can filter and read the last updated column via the API, but you currently cannot update or clear it.Filtering the last updated columnUsing the items_page object, you can easily...

image1485×391 21.7 KB

Thanks & Regards,
Madhu Kolli

posts.author: madhusudhana.kolli (Madhu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Using below GraphQL , I am able to fetch only incremental records .
compare_value: [“EXACT”, “2024-03-21”] needs to parameterised .

{
boards(ids: 123) {
id
name
items_page(
limit: 500
–query_params: {rules: [{column_id: “last_updated”, compare_value: [“EXACT”, “2024-03-21”], operator:greater_than_or_equals,compare_attribute:“UPDATED_AT”}]}
) {
cursor
items {
item_id: id
Project: name
item_create_date: created_at
item_last_update_date: updated_at
group {
id
group_name: title
}
subitems {
subitem_id: id
subproject: name
column_values {
column {
title
}
text
value
}
}
column_values {
… on MirrorValue {
display_value
}
column {
title
}
text
value
}
}

}

}
}

posts.author: sealv_wsm (Daniel Alvarado)
posts.cookedHtml:
This works Madhu, I just don’t know exactly how you got to that “EXACT” operator in the compare_values. I didn’t see anything similar in the Documentation: Last updated

What is the logic?

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Daniel,

You can have a look at the Date column type documentation on how to use operators with different types of compare values. This works with the last updated column as well. Good luck!

Reinier

posts.author: madhusudhana.kolli (Madhu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, it’s not very clear in their documentation.

But, I tried multiple versions and finally got this solution.

Hope this helps for many.

Thanks & Regards,
Madhu Kolli

posts.author: reformy (Yair Ben Meir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @madhusudhana.kolli ! That was a heard one to find a solution to.
Let also say that the one should pass date as you did. If you pass a date and a time (as I did…) it will ignore the time part.
